title: Prefer union type expressions over typing.Union in type hints
description: Using typing.Union can lead to unexpected behavior when checking subtypes, whereas union type expressions are more explicit and can help catch type errors.
message: Prefer union type expressions over typing.Union in type hints

noncompliant.code:
from typing import Union

def func(arg: Union[int, str]): pass

compliant.code:
from typing import TypeVar, Literal

TypeAlias = TypeVar('TypeAlias', int, str)

DefinedType = Literal['int', 'str']

def func(arg: TypeAlias) -> DefinedType: ...
compliant.description: Uses union type expressions with TypeVar and Literal

why_is_this_an_issue: Using typing.Union can lead to unexpected behavior when checking subtypes, whereas union type expressions are more explicit and can help catch type errors.
how_to_fix: Use union type expressions with TypeVar and Literal
